compiled_state.title: Implement ticket-lib.sh forward-compat reader and schema_version derivation in _write_preconditions
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Context
Story 86ad-f5e7. Extends ticket-lib.sh with forward-compat reader behavior and schema_version/manifest_depth fields written by _write_preconditions(). Must make T3 (UPDATE tests) pass. Depends on T3 (UPDATE tests), T2 (classifier exists), and T6 (contract defines schema_version rules).

**Note on Story 1 compatibility**: _write_preconditions() signature established in Story 1: `_write_preconditions(ticket_id, stage, depth, payload)`. The `depth` parameter is already in the signature — this task uses it to derive schema_version and sets manifest_depth in the event JSON. No signature change.

## Implementation

Modify: `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh`

### Change 1: _write_preconditions() — set schema_version and manifest_depth from depth param

In `_write_preconditions()`, after receiving the `depth` parameter:
```bash
# Derive schema_version from depth (per preconditions-schema-v2 contract)
case "$depth" in
    minimal) local schema_version=1 ;;
    standard|deep) local schema_version=2 ;;
    *) local schema_version=1 ;;  # fail-open on unknown depth
esac
```

Inject `schema_version` and `manifest_depth` ("$depth") into the event JSON payload before persisting.

### Change 2: _read_latest_preconditions() — forward-compat unknown schema_version handling

After reading the event JSON:
```bash
# Forward-compat: unknown schema_version → log once per (ticket, version), fall back to minimal
local event_schema_version
event_schema_version=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('schema_version', 1))" < "$event_file" 2>/dev/null || echo 1)

if [ "$event_schema_version" -gt 2 ] 2>/dev/null; then
    # Unknown future version — warn once per (ticket, version) using a state file
    local warn_key="${ticket_id}_v${event_schema_version}"
    local warn_state_dir="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-preconditions-warn"
    mkdir -p "$warn_state_dir"
    if [ ! -f "$warn_state_dir/$warn_key" ]; then
        echo "[DSO WARN] preconditions reader: unknown schema_version=${event_schema_version} for ticket ${ticket_id} — falling back to minimal-tier interpretation" >&2
        touch "$warn_state_dir/$warn_key"
    fi
    # Fall back: return only minimal-tier fields from the event
fi
```

### Forward-compat contract invariants (must hold after this task):
- Unknown schema_version: never reject; always return minimal-tier fields; log once per (ticket, version)
- schema_version=1: minimal tier (Story 1 events)
- schema_version=2: standard or deep tier; manifest_depth discriminates
- schema_version > 2: treat as unknown; log + fall back to minimal

## File Impact

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh` | Modify (_write_preconditions + _read_latest_preconditions) |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All T3 tests (forward-compat reader + schema_version writer tests) pass
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-ticket-lib.sh 2>&1 | grep "FAILED: 0"
- [ ] _write_preconditions sets schema_version=1 for minimal depth
  Verify: verified via T3 test test_writer_schema_version_minimal
- [ ] _write_preconditions sets schema_version=2 for standard/deep depth
  Verify: verified via T3 tests test_writer_schema_version_standard and test_writer_schema_version_deep
- [ ] _read_latest_preconditions logs warning for unknown schema_version ≥ 3 (once per ticket+version)
  Verify: verified via T3 test test_forward_compat_reader_unknown_schema_version_logs_warning
- [ ] _read_latest_preconditions does NOT reject unknown schema_version event (exit 0)
  Verify: verified via T3 test test_forward_compat_reader_unknown_schema_version_no_rejection
- [ ] No change to existing function signatures (backward-compatible)
  Verify: git diff HEAD -- plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib.sh | grep "^-.*_write_preconditions\|^-.*_read_latest_preconditions" | grep -v "#" | wc -l | awk '{exit ($1 > 0)}'
- [ ] check-plugin-self-ref.sh passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-plugin-self-ref.sh
- [ ] check-portability.sh passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit/check-portability.sh

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately

comments.body: AC amendment: _write_preconditions() MUST overwrite schema_version in the event JSON with the depth-derived value even if the caller includes schema_version in the payload — this ensures the contract source of truth is always the depth parameter, not the caller's discretion. Add verification: written event file schema_version matches depth-derived value regardless of payload input.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: AC amendment: Warning deduplication scope — the once-per-(ticket,version) warning is per process session (state in $TMPDIR). Document this scope explicitly in the code comment: 'dedup is session-scoped; a repeat warning on re-invocation is acceptable and expected (informational only)'. T3 Test 15 verifies deduplication within a single process run.
comments.author: Test
